decisions.summary: Chose option (b) — explicit "parent" sentinel — over option (a) UI-only. A sub-role model of "parent" is resolved by resolveModel to the parent role's model. Rationale: self-documenting in raw config.yaml; avoids the implicit "absent/empty {} sub-config means inherit" magic that option (a) would require; and option (a) is not actually UI-only because settings-api.ts validateModelRef rejects an absent model field. The #1201 collision flagged in the issue is moot — PAN-1201 already merged (commit 817c2c713).

"parent" is valid ONLY in roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model. It is rejected for role-level roles.<role>.model and for workhorses.<slot>. resolveModel treats a sub-role model of "parent" as equivalent to an absent sub-role model, falling through to roleModel ?? DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role].

resolveModel is the single model-resolution point. Intercepting "parent" there covers every runtime caller (agents.ts, review-agent.ts, inspect-agent.ts, specialist-context.ts, swarm/specialists/flywheel routes, etc.) with no per-caller change.
Each code bead ships with its own unit tests rather than splitting tests into separate test-only beads. This keeps every bead independently reviewable and testable in isolation.

Role-level "Parent" inheritance is out of scope. Roles (plan/work/review/test/ship/flywheel) have no parent role; "parent" applies only to sub-roles. The role-level model picker does not gain a "Parent" option.

hazards.summary: derefWorkhorse would silently return a bogus model id if it ever received the literal "parent" (isWorkhorseRef is false, resolveModelId passes it through unchecked).
hazards.mitigation: resolveModel normalizes a sub-role "parent" to undefined before calling derefWorkhorse, and validateRoleModelRefs skips derefWorkhorse for sub-role configs whose model is "parent". Additionally add a fail-fast guard in derefWorkhorse that throws a clear error if "parent" reaches it — it must be resolved away upstream, never dereferenced.

hazards.summary: mergeConfigs calls validateRoleModelRefs at load time (config-yaml.ts:1585). A config with a sub-role model of "parent" must not throw there, or every config load fails.
hazards.mitigation: validateRoleModelRefs skips the derefWorkhorse call for sub-role entries whose model equals "parent".

hazards.summary: The frontend ModelPicker component in RolesPanel.tsx is shared between the role-level picker and the sub-role pickers. The "Parent" option must appear ONLY in sub-role pickers.
hazards.mitigation: Gate the "Parent" option on a new optional parentModelRef prop. Only the sub-role call site passes it; the role-level call site omits it.

hazards.summary: ModelPicker helpers (isWorkhorseRef, modelExists / currentSpecificModelMissing, displayModelRef, modelRefTooltip, resolveModelRef, providerWarning) assume the value is a workhorse ref or a concrete model id. "parent" is neither and would be flagged as a missing specific model or produce a spurious provider warning.
hazards.mitigation: Special-case the "parent" value in those helpers so it is never flagged as missing, the tooltip/Resolved line shows the parent role's effective model, and no invalid-provider warning fires.

hazards.summary: The dashboard frontend does not import from src/lib, so it cannot reuse the PARENT_MODEL_REF constant defined in config-yaml.ts.
hazards.mitigation: Define a local "parent" literal in RolesPanel.tsx and keep it identical to PARENT_MODEL_REF. The settings-api round-trip test (bead settings-validation) and the resolver test (bead core-resolver) both anchor the canonical string, so drift surfaces in CI.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1382: Sub-role models: add a 'Parent' option (inherit the parent role's model)
